container.title: Rules
container.description:
You must complete units as specified in Parts A, B and C (144 points): a minimum of 96 points of arts listed study over Part A and Part B, and 48 points of free electives in Part C.

To satisfy the requirements of the Bachelor of Arts you must complete 144 credit points as specified in the course requirements, including:

• a maximum of 60 credit points at Level 1; and
• a minimum of 36 credit points at Level 3, of which at least 24 credit points must be Arts units.

Units are 6 points unless otherwise specified.

container.title: Part A. Major studies
European languages extended major
container.description: You have the option of reaching proficient level in two European Languages by completing an extended major. You must begin at entry level 2 intermediate level in at least one of the two languages.

parent_record.value: Curriculum Structure Container: European languages extended major
Curriculum Structure Container: Part A. Major studies

container.title: APAC  - Psychology extended  major
container.description:
The Psychology extended major (APAC accredited level 1 sequence of units) is only offered in the single degree A2000 Bachelor of Arts or in the double degree S2006 Bachelor of Science and Bachelor of Arts.

If you are completing the Psychology extended major (APAC accredited level 1 sequence of units) below you will be required to complete 60 credit points of Psychology units and 36 credit points of Arts units which must include Arts electives (12 credit points) and Professional Futures domain units (24 credit points).

parent_record.value: Curriculum Structure Container: APAC  - Psychology extended  major
Curriculum Structure Container: Part A. Major studies

container.description:
You must complete one of the following majors including:

• a maximum of 12 credit points at Level 1; and
• a minimum of 18 credit points at Level 3.

Note:
• It is recommended that you complete a sequence of level 1 units in more than one Arts major area in order to have options for finalising your choice of major.

• If you intend to complete the Economics major, the background knowledge and application of maths is required at the equivalent VCE Year 12 Higher level. You may have satisfied this by completing relevant prerequisite unit/s, or by studying relevant topics in your final years of secondary school or by successfully completing either ECC1550 Introduction to quantitative skills for social sciences or MTH1010 Functions and their applications, as a first-year elective

• The European languages extended major requires entry level 2 intermediate in at least one of two language options.

• The Psychology extended major (APAC accredited level 1 sequence of units) is only offered in:
- A2000 Bachelor of Arts; and
- S2006 Bachelor of Science and Bachelor of Arts.

• If you intend to complete honours in your major area, you should check the required pre-requisites (e.g., specific units/credit points etc) to ensure you meet eligibility requirements.

container.title: Part B. Specified studies
Professional Futures
Global immersion
container.description:
You may complete the following units or any of the Arts study tours or semester length exchange or study abroad program.

Please visit https://www.monash.edu/arts/learning-abroad/global-study-tours for more information.

container.description:
You must complete 24 credit points from the units in the following Professional Futures domains.

Note: If you are enrolled in the double degree D3002 Bachelor of Education (Honours) in Secondary Education and Bachelor of Arts, you must complete 12 credit points of units from the Professional Futures domains and the remaining 12 credit points should be selected from level 1, 2 or 3 Arts' elective units.

For your discipline studies in the double degree secondary education specialisation, you must complete a Victorian Institute of Teaching minor study in one secondary teaching area. The minor study must consist of four units, with no more than two units at first year level. Space for the minor study units will come from elective unit allocations on the course map. Approved disciplines can be found on the secondary specialist areas page.
.

container.title: Discipline elective studies
container.description: You must complete 24 credit points of Discipline elective studies which can be combined into a minor of study different to the chosen major from Part A and with no more than 12 credit points at Level 1.

container.description:
You must complete:

- 24 credit points of Discipline elective studies which can be combined into a minor in an area of study different to the chosen arts major from Part A.; and
- 24 credit points of units from the Professional Futures domains.

container.title: Part C. Free elective studies
container.description:
You may choose electives from units offered within the Bachelor of Arts, or any other Arts undergraduate course*. Using your electives in this way, may assist you to complete:
• an extended Arts major
• an additional Arts major or minors
• an Arts Bachelor + Masters accelerated pathway
• additional Arts global study tour opportunities
• additional Arts industry experience opportunities

Note: no more than two units (12 credit points) can be credited towards two majors, or a major and a minor. The same credit points cannot be credited towards more than one minor.

You may also use your free electives to explore different disciplines and experiences offered from across Monash. This includes taking undergraduate units^, minors or majors offered by other Faculties* and participating in University programs* such as Flagship Rich Educational Experiences (FREE).

Elective units may be at any level ensuring that the course rules are satisfied for the Bachelor of Arts.

* subject to unit rules and enrolment restrictions
^ undergraduate units are those that commence with the numbers 1-3; the level of the unit is indicated by the first number in the unit code.
